statement.p: Matteo is designing a 55-inch TV, which means that the diagonal of the TVs screen will be 55 inches long.

image.shortdescription: a rectangle representing the TV with a diagonal line drawn and labeled c=55 in;

statement.p: He needs the screens width-to-height ratio to be 16:9\text{.} Find the TV screens width and height.

solution.p: Let x represent the height of the screen, in inches. Since the screens width-to-height ratio will be 16:9\text{,} then the width is \frac{16}{9} times as long as the height. The width is \frac{16}{9}x inches. We can draw a diagram.

image.shortdescription: The previous image of the TV with the height labeled x in and the base width labeled 16/9 in

solution.p: Now we can use the Pythagorean Theorem to write and solve an equation:
Since the screens height is {26.9644\ {\rm in}}\text{,} its width is \frac{16}{9}26.9643681797907=2297.92284866469 inches.
